text.title: django-pytest Core Rules
text.summary: Six rules for the modern Django test stack on pytest.

rule.statement: Use `pytest + pytest-django`. Do not use Django's `unittest`-style `TestCase` for new tests. Existing TestCase classes are migrated incrementally.
rule.rationale: pytest fixtures compose cleanly; setUp/tearDown chains do not. The ecosystem (factory_boy, hypothesis, syrupy) integrates with pytest, not unittest.

rule.statement: Test data is created in `@pytest.fixture` (in `conftest.py` or per-module). NO `setUp` / `setUpTestData` methods. Composition via fixture parameter lists.
rule.rationale: Fixtures are reusable across tests/modules without inheritance; setUp couples data to one TestCase subtree.

rule.statement: Test data is built with `factory_boy` (preferred for explicit factories) or `model_bakery` (preferred for terse one-offs). NO `Model.objects.create()` inline in tests.
rule.rationale: Factories centralise defaults; bakery generates plausible values. Inline `.create()` produces brittle setup that breaks every model field change.

rule.statement: Tests that touch the DB declare `db` (or `django_db`) fixture explicitly OR use `@pytest.mark.django_db`. No accidental DB usage.
rule.rationale: Explicit DB markers make the slow-tests boundary visible. Without them, fast tests get accidentally promoted to integration-tier latency.

rule.statement: DRF tests use `APIClient` + `client.force_authenticate(user=user)`. Do NOT call the login endpoint inside every test setup.
rule.rationale: force_authenticate skips the auth round-trip; tests run an order of magnitude faster and isolate the unit under test.

rule.statement: Mocks live ONLY at process boundaries: outbound HTTP, S3, third-party SDKs, email. Internal services / utils are called for real (in-memory DB).
rule.rationale: Mocking internal services tests the mocks, not the code. Boundary-only mocking keeps tests honest while staying fast.
